mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5 percent, rising consumer credit balances indicate excessive leverage that eventually defaults, causing equity drawdowns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5 percent and RSAFS rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0 percent annualized over two consecutive quarters, it signals a demand shock that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.0
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances despite the GDP growth threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates indicate a tightening labor market that accelerates wage inflation, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy and compressing equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary regime where CPI growth falls below 1.5 percent year-over-year, a decline in housing starts signals collapsing consumer confidence and weak aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth falls below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL growth is below 1.5 percent and HOUST declines.
